news.title: More military technologies targeted for civilian sector to boost economy
news.summary: China will strengthen innovation by developing high-tech industries with military technologies to boost military-civil integration, a move that aims to cultivate new growth drivers and boost the economy, said a recent State Council guideline.

news.title: 特朗普：若无法与伊朗达成协议，将以更大力度恢复军事行动，美方已为军舰装载“最先进的武器” - thepaper.cn
news.summary: 特朗普：若无法与伊朗达成协议，将以更大力度恢复军事行动，美方已为军舰装载“最先进的武器”  thepaper.cn

news.title: 伊朗代表团：我们心怀善意，但不信任美国
news.summary:
据伊朗国家电视台今天（4月11日）报道，伊朗伊斯兰议会议长卡利巴夫在率团抵达巴基斯坦伊斯兰堡机场后表示，“我们心怀善意，但不信任美国。我们与美国谈判总是以失败告终，他们总是违背协议。不到一年的时间里，他们已两次在谈判进行到一半时袭击我们，并犯下多项战争罪行。”

卡利巴夫还表示，在即将进行的谈判中，如果美方愿意达成真正的协议，并给予伊朗人民应有的权利，美国将会看到伊朗达成协议的诚意。（总台记者 李健南 曲晟）

news.title: 美媒：美伊谈判在即，美军继续增兵中东
news.summary:
新华社华盛顿4月10日电 据美国《华尔街日报》10日报道，美国和伊朗即将在伊斯兰堡开始谈判之际，美军继续向中东地区部署战机和部队，为后续可能的军事行动做准备。

报道以飞行追踪数据和美国官员为消息源报道，多架战斗机和攻击机已于近期抵达中东，预计来自美国陆军第82空降师的1500至2000名士兵今后数日内也将抵达。同时，数千名海军水兵和海军陆战队员正在前往该地区途中。

另据美海军官员透露，3月底从美国弗吉尼亚州出发的“乔治·H·W·布什”号航空母舰及其护航舰艇，目前正航行在大西洋海域；3月中旬从加利福尼亚州出发的“拳师”号两栖攻击舰及其护航舰艇编队，搭载着第11海军陆战队远征队，正航行在太平洋海域。这些舰船可能需要一周以上时间才能抵达中东。

news.title: 美伊今日谈判，双方强硬表态，24小时成关键窗口
news.summary: 当地时间11日凌晨，巴基斯坦官方确认，美伊谈判将于当天在巴首都伊斯兰堡的塞雷纳酒店举行。伊朗代表团已经抵达伊斯兰堡。美国副总统万斯则于10日早些时候启程前往伊斯兰堡。美伊双方均就谈判发出强硬表态。

news.title: 以驻美大使称并未同意与黎真主党讨论停火问题
news.summary:
当地时间4月11日，总台记者获悉，以色列驻美国大使叶希尔·莱特表示，以色列同意于14日与黎巴嫩举行会谈，但并未同意与黎真主党讨论停火问题。他表示，“真主党仍在持续袭击以色列，这是和平进程的主要障碍”。

黎巴嫩总统府10日晚发表声明说，黎巴嫩和以色列同意14日在美国国务院举行首次会议，讨论宣布停火以及在美国主持下启动谈判的日期。（总台记者 梁慧 孙建）

news.title: 伊朗代表团抵达伊斯兰堡
news.summary:
新华社德黑兰4月10日电 （记者陈霄 沙达提）德黑兰当地时间10日深夜，伊朗媒体报道，由伊朗伊斯兰议会议长卡利巴夫率领的伊朗代表团抵达巴基斯坦伊斯兰堡，将与美方进行谈判。

报道说，代表团成员涵盖政治、安全、经济等多领域的高级官员，包括伊朗外交部长阿拉格齐、防务委员会秘书阿里·阿克巴尔·艾哈迈迪安、中央银行行长阿卜杜勒纳赛尔·赫马提等。

报道说，如伊方提出的相关前提得到满足，谈判将正式启动。伊朗强调，如果未能达成令伊朗以及“抵抗之弧”满意的协议，冲突将重新爆发。

巴基斯坦总理夏巴兹10日在电视讲话中说，美国与伊朗代表团将于11日在巴首都伊斯兰堡举行谈判，以寻求和平解决当前中东危机的方案。

news.title: 特朗普称霍尔木兹海峡“很快就会重新开放”
news.summary: 当地时间4月10日，美国总统特朗普表示，霍尔木兹海峡“很快就会重新开放，无论如何都会开放”。（央视记者 许骁）

news.title: 黎以双方同意14日在美讨论停火
news.summary: 新华社贝鲁特4月10日电 （记者申峰 曹昌浩）黎巴嫩总统府10日晚发表声明说，黎巴嫩和以色列同意14日在美国国务院举行首次会议，讨论宣布停火以及在美国主持下启动谈判的日期。

news.title: 美伊谈判时间形式仍未知，媒体中心已启用
news.summary: 美国与伊朗代表团预计将于4月11日在巴基斯坦首都伊斯兰堡举行谈判。目前，伊斯兰堡加强了安保，媒体中心开始启用。但据总

news.title: 伊朗警告将有力回应对黎巴嫩的持续袭击
news.summary:
新华社德黑兰4月10日电 伊朗武装部队哈塔姆安比亚中央总部10日发表声明说，如果敌方持续对黎巴嫩发动袭击，伊朗将予以有力回应。

声明说，伊朗将继续掌握对霍尔木兹海峡的控制权，如果敌方持续对黎巴嫩真主党及黎巴嫩人民发动袭击，伊朗将予以有力回应。

声明指出，鉴于美国和以色列方面屡次违背承诺，伊朗武装力量始终将“指头放在扳机上”。

news.title: 以军空袭黎南部一政府大楼致13名安全人员死亡
news.summary:
黎巴嫩国家安全总局10日发表声明说，以色列当天空袭黎南部城市纳巴提耶政府大楼，造成13名安全人员死亡。

声明说，以军袭击了纳巴提耶政府大楼，该大楼内设有国家安全总局纳巴提耶地区分局。

黎总统约瑟夫·奥恩发表声明谴责以方袭击，并强调以色列袭击黎巴嫩国家机构和安全部门并不会阻止其行使捍卫领土和主权的权利。他再次呼吁国际社会承担责任，采取行动制止以色列的袭击行为。

news.title: 司机开路虎加油后逃单，江西警方发布警情通报
郑丽文：只要对两岸和平有帮助的事，都愿意去做
news.summary:
新华社北京4月10日电 （记者赵博、王承昊）10日下午，正在大陆参访的中国国民党访问团在北京举行记者会。中国国民党主席郑丽文应询表示，未来要坚定地走和平之路，“只要对两岸和平有帮助的事，我都愿意去做”。

应中共中央和习近平总书记邀请，郑丽文一行于4月7日至12日到访大陆。记者会上，郑丽文回顾了连日来在南京、上海的参访经历，盛赞大陆方面贴心的安排接待。她特别提及，国共两党领导人时隔10年再次会面，真情流露、坦率诚挚，充分展现了共同期待两岸关系和平发展的心愿、善意与诚意。

“两岸关系日趋紧张，恶意螺旋上升，是大家不愿意看到的。”郑丽文答问时表示，担任中国国民党主席以来，感受到岛内百工百业对加强两岸交流、维护台海和平的强烈呼声。此次大陆行，证明没有什

news.title: 新华社：别让功利主义毁掉中国足球的未来
news.summary:
新华社北京4月10日电 体育时评：别让功利主义毁掉中国足球的未来

新华社记者肖世尧、王浩宇、李春宇

近日，2026年第五届中国青少年足球联赛北京赛区男子U10组爆出严重虚假比赛事件。通报显示，东城体校16橙队与踢球者兰队在比赛中心照不宣互射乌龙，门将故意放水，行径荒唐。

消息一出，舆论哗然。不到十岁的孩子，为何要把球往自家门里踢？

真相并不复杂，却足够刺眼：两队赛前均已提前出线，根据赛程，获胜一方将在淘汰赛中遭遇实力更强的对手。于是，双方教练竟不约而同选择了“主动输球”。更令人愤慨的是，现场的裁判员与比赛监督也未及时制止这种明显违背体育道德的行为，任由闹剧继续。

不合理的赛制设计、形同虚设的现场监督、扭曲错

news.title: 西班牙呼吁欧盟成员国暂停与以色列的联系国协定
news.summary:

桑切斯表示，暂停与以色列之间的联系国协定是“为了保持一致

news.title: 特朗普称若未达成协议，将加大对伊朗军事打击
news.summary:

当地时间4月10日，央视记者获悉，美国总统特朗普表示，若无法与伊朗达成和平协议，美国将以更大力度恢复军事行动。

特朗普在接受采访时称，美方正在“重新部署”，并已为军舰装载“最先进的武器”，若谈

news.title: 中国驻伊朗大使馆：再次提醒中国公民暂勿前往伊朗
news.summary:
近日美国与伊朗达成停火、即将开启谈判，但地区局势仍存在很大不确定性，伊朗安全形势依然严峻复杂。外交部已提醒中国公民暂勿前往伊朗，中国驻伊朗使领馆提醒中国公民严格遵守相关安全提醒，已在当地的中国公民减少不必要外出，避免前往敏感区域。如遇紧急情况，请及时报警并向中国驻伊朗使领馆寻求协助。

如中国公民坚持前往伊朗，有可能面临极高安全风险，并可能影响其获得协助的实效。

联系方式:

伊朗急救电话：115

伊朗报警电话：110

外交部全球领事保护与服务应急热线（24小时）：

+86-10-12308

+86-10-65612308

驻伊朗使馆领事保护与协助电话：

+98-9

news.title: Lebanon and Israel officials to meet in US on Tuesday
news.summary: A senior Lebanese official earlier told the BBC that Lebanon would only take part in talks if a ceasefire was in place.

news.title: The five big sticking points in US-Iran talks
news.summary: Peace talks between the US and Iran are due to begin in Pakistan on Saturday.

news.title: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
news.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

news.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda locked in vicious 'civil war', say researchers
news.summary: The once close-knit community of Ngogo chimpanzees have been at loggerheads for the last eight years.

news.title: Gunmen kill at least four people at Afghanistan picnic spot
news.summary: Officials say attackers opened fire near the village of Deh Mehri in Herat.

news.title: French man charged with locking son in van for more than a year
news.summary: The boy was found malnourished, naked, lying next to human excrement and unable to walk, the prosecutor said.

news.title: South Korea deploys thermal imaging cameras in search for escaped zoo wolf
news.summary: The male wolf, named Neukgu, escaped a zoo in the central city of Daejeon on Wednesday, officials said.

news.title: Melania Trump's speech propels Epstein crisis back to forefront
news.summary: She has now placed herself squarely into the Epstein story and at odds with the administration, which wants to end the investigation.

news.title: US Democratic lawmaker Raskin seeks commission to oversee removal of presidents
news.summary: U.S. Representative Jamie Raskin of Maryland told fellow House Democrats on Friday that he will introduce legislation creating a commission to facilitate the removal of any president deemed unable to carry out their duties, according ‌to a House Judiciary Committee spokeswoman.

news.title: Trump says US will have Strait of Hormuz 'open fairly soon'
news.summary: President Donald Trump told reporters on Friday that the U.S. will have the Strait of Hormuz "open ‌fairly soon" but did not elaborate and acknowledged that it will not be an easy step.

news.title: North Korea's Kim meets China's foreign minister, calls for stronger ties, KCNA says
news.summary: North Korean leader Kim Jong ‌Un met China's foreign minister, state media KCNA said ⁠on Saturday.

news.title: Explainer: Why are people talking about the 25th Amendment to the US Constitution?
news.summary: President Donald Trump's profanity-laced threats to wipe out Iran's civilization have led some Democrats to discuss attempting to remove him from office by using the 25th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution.

news.title: US team heads to Iran talks in Pakistan with low expectations
news.summary: A U.S. team led by Vice President JD Vance left for Islamabad on Friday for weekend talks with Iran, even as both sides accused each other of breaking commitments made to secure a temporary ceasefire.

news.title: Colombia raises Ecuador tariffs to 100% as trade tensions escalate
news.summary: Colombia will raise tariffs on neighboring Ecuador to 100% from 30%, Colombia's trade ministry said on Friday, matching Ecuador's measure from a ‌day earlier amid an escalating trade and diplomatic spat between the two South American nations.

news.title: FAA, Pentagon sign agreement on deploying anti-drone laser system near Mexico
news.summary: The Federal Aviation Administration and Pentagon said on Friday they had signed an agreement allowing the government's use of a high-energy laser counter-drone system along the southern ‌U.S. border with Mexico.

news.title: US House Oversight chair vows hearings with Epstein victims after Melania Trump's speech
news.summary: U.S. Representative James Comer, the chairman of the House Oversight Committee, said on Friday he agreed with first lady Melania Trump's call for congressional hearings with alleged victims of Jeffrey ‌Epstein, saying "we will have hearings."

news.title: White House opted against televised address about Iran ceasefire, US officials say
news.summary: The White House considered but decided against a national televised address by President Donald Trump on Tuesday about his ceasefire deal with Iran, with some aides and advisers privately voicing concern about potentially overselling the still-nascent agreement, three U.S. officials told Reuters.

news.title: Exclusive: ICE launches new effort to uncover US ‘birth tourism schemes’
news.summary: President Donald Trump’s administration plans to crack down on networks it says help pregnant women lie on visa applications in order to secure U.S. citizenship for their U.S.-born babies, an issue ‌that Trump has highlighted to justify his attempts to restrict birthright citizenship.

news.title: Kamala Harris says she might run for president again in 2028
news.summary: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, who was the Democratic Party's nominee for the presidency in 2024, said on Friday that she was considering running for president ‌again in 2028.

news.title: Iran wanted to negotiate with Vance. They got their wish
news.summary: When JD Vance arrives in Islamabad for talks on Saturday with Iranian officials, it will fulfill a wish for Tehran's remaining leaders, some of whom have quietly sought the U.S. vice president to take a lead role in negotiations to end the war, according to several sources familiar with the matter.

news.title: Lebanon heads to historic Israel talks with few hopes except to staunch bloodshed
news.summary: Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun has called for historic direct talks with longtime foe Israel since war erupted a month ago - a month in which Israel's military has forced more than a million Lebanese to flee, levelled parts of Beirut and triggered sectarian friction.

news.title: UK police arrest Sudanese man after four die in Channel boat crossing
news.summary: British police have arrested a Sudanese man on suspicion of endangering life after four migrants died while attempting to cross the ‌Channel from France to Britain on a small boat this week, authorities said.

news.title: One dead, 27 injured as bus carrying British tourists crashes in Spain's La Gomera
news.summary: At least one person died and 27 were injured after a ‌bus carrying British tourists crashed down a ravine on the Spanish island of La Gomera, emergency services said on Friday.

news.title: Iran War Live Updates: Iranian Delegation Arrives at Peace Talks, With Vance en Route
news.summary: There was much uncertainty about the durability of the cease-fire and whether the two sides could reach a long-term deal, as Vice President JD Vance traveled to Pakistan for the talks.

news.title: Prince Harry Is Sued for Libel by a Charity He Founded
news.summary: Sentebale filed a defamation lawsuit against the Duke of Sussex, who resigned as patron last year after a clash between the board of trustees and the board’s chair.

news.title: ‘I’m Fed Up.’ Frustrated With Trump, Starmer Embraces Other Allies.
news.summary: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain is strengthening ties in Europe and the Middle East as the once-special relationship with America sours.

news.title: Should Churches Remove Art by a Priest Accused of Sexual Abuse?
news.summary: The Rev. Marko Ivan Rupnik has installed mosaics across the Roman Catholic world, including at the Vatican. After nuns accused him of abuse, some want his work removed.

news.title: Iran’s South Africa Mission Trolls Trump Amid Hormuz Ceasefire
news.summary: Official social media accounts for Iranian embassies have engaged in information warfare, but the ones in Africa appear to have taken the lead on the attacks.

news.title: What the Cease-Fire Means for Iran
news.summary: Emerging from weeks of U.S. and Israeli airstrikes, an emboldened Iran has 10 demands for talks during the tenuous cease-fire, according to Iranian state media. Our reporter Erika Solomon assesses Iran’s position.

news.title: University of Ottawa in Lockdown Amid ‘Violent Incident’
news.summary: The authorities urged students to stay away from the downtown campus, to hide quietly if they were on the premises, and to defend themselves if needed.

news.title: New Evidence Further Implicates U.S. Missiles in Strikes That Killed 21 Civilians in Iran
news.summary: Additional images and video build on an earlier analysis, which the Pentagon has disputed, showing Precision Strike Missiles, or PrSMs, hit a sports hall and residential areas in the Iranian city of Lamerd.

news.title: Trump Is Racing to Redefine ‘America First’ in a Time of War
news.summary: Washington has been debating the thrust of Mr. Trump’s foreign policy for more than a decade, with the only consensus being that “America First” means whatever the president says it does.

news.title: With U.S.-Iran Peace Talks Set to Begin, Prospects for Long-Term Deal Uncertain
news.summary: With U.S. and Iranian leaders heading to Pakistan for negotiations amid a two-week cease-fire, the two sides must resolve major differences on Lebanon, the Strait of Hormuz and frozen Iranian assets.

news.title: Facing Many Crises, Pakistan Tries to End a Big One — in Iran
news.summary: A nation with a stalled economy, a terrorism problem and two hostile neighbors is set to host the first formal U.S.-Iranian talks since the war began.

news.title: Why Opening the Strait of Hormuz Won’t Immediately Lower Gas Prices
news.summary: Our energy reporter Rebecca F. Elliott explains why, even if the flow of energy is restored through the Persian Gulf, it will take months to carry out repairs across dozens of energy sites in the region.

news.title: Only two ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Friday.
Israel and Lebanon Plan Talks in D.C. as War Threatens U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Deal
news.summary: Whether Israel’s conflict with Hezbollah, the Iran-backed militant group in Lebanon, is included in the Iran cease-fire is one of the major disputes rattling the truce.

news.title: Iran Demands Release of ‘Blocked Assets’ Before Peace Talks
news.summary: In what appeared to be a new condition, Iran’s parliament speaker said that negotiations set for Saturday in Pakistan would not go ahead unless unspecified Iranian funds were released.

news.title: Fuel Protests Cause Transport Chaos in Ireland as Iran War Spikes Prices
news.summary: The Irish government said it had called in the army after protesters blocked highways, ports and an oil refinery, causing widespread disruption.

news.title: Why Investing in Wind and Solar to Avoid Gas Shocks Hasn’t Added Up for Some
news.summary: Renewable energy is cheaper to run than fossil fuels, especially with war choking oil supply. But it hasn’t turned out that way for some European countries, and the reason is complex.

news.title: Middle East War Triggers Higher Prices in China
news.summary: Three and a half years of deflationary pressure on Chinese factories reversed course last month as higher energy prices cycled into the economy.

news.title: Here’s the latest.

news.summary: Vice President JD Vance will lead the United States delegation which is set to meet with Iranian officials in Pakistan on Saturday.

news.summary:

「我是一个，鬼才。」
「但我要的是一个天才！天才！天才知道什么意思吗？！」
「知道，就是…天生的，鬼才。」
「我…有没有搞错啊？！」

news.title: 带你穿越回梵高割耳那晚，原来这么多细节都是谣言！
news.summary:
关于梵高割耳的故事，有无数的传言。
有人觉得梵高本来就疯了，有人说梵高是为了一个妓女割下了耳朵，甚至有人说梵高和高更是同性恋人关系。
这些说法，哪个才是真相？
我们一直默认梵高就是疯子，却忘了：
一个人要经历了怎样的绝望，才会用刀对准自己。
这期视频为大家破解那62天的隐秘细节、还原梵高割耳的真相。

news.title: 周深X王者荣耀世界《有你在的世界》MV上线
news.summary:
若时间是一条奔涌的长河，愿每一次汇聚，我们都能认出彼此。
若人生是一场无尽的旅途，愿每一场冒险，都有英雄并肩而立。
我和@王者荣耀世界  合作的开服主题曲《有你在的世界》MV与同名专辑在今日正式上线。
世界闪耀的星光，是你陪在我身旁。这一次，让我们一起奔向旅途的晴朗吧！

news.title: 《崩坏：星穹铁道》4.2版本「如是，众生欢笑不已」前瞻特别节目
news.summary:

本次特别节目中的一切人物、故事、设定仅为节目包装，均与《崩坏：星穹铁道》原作无关，展示画面与奖励等均为研发中内容，非最终上线版本，请以上线版本为准。
亲爱的开拓者，本次特别节目中的兑换码将于2026年4月11日23:59:59失效，记得尽快兑换哦~
